prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "驴踢腿" (donkey kick / quadruped hip extension) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the top of a donkey kick: in a quadruped position with hands flat on the ground directly under shoulders and right knee on the ground directly under hip; left leg lifted with knee still bent at 90 degrees and the sole of the left foot pushing toward the ceiling, thigh parallel to the ground or slightly higher; spine neutral, hips and shoulders level (no rotation); plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus maximus of the kicking (left) leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hamstring group of the kicking leg and core muscles for stability. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus of the kicking leg; "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group; "核心肌群" pointing to the core muscles. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
